    • Running Analysis Pipelines

    Getting Started with utilrep2

    getting-started.Rmd

    Overview

    utilrep2 provides an R interface to the correlates_reporting2 pipeline — a multi-module system for vaccine correlates-of-risk (CoR), correlates-of-protection (CoP), and immunogenicity analyses.

    Instead of navigating to each module directory and typing make, you can orchestrate every step from an interactive R session or an R script.

    Installation

    Install the package from the project source:

    # From the project root
install.packages(".", repos = NULL, type = "source")

    Or load it in development mode with devtools:

    devtools::load_all()

    Quick-start: setup_session()

    The recommended way to start a session is setup_session(). Two settings are required before any run_*() function will execute:

    Argument Required What it sets
    platform yes Script routing: "linux", "win", or "auto"
    data_path yes Absolute path to the analysis data folder
    trial no TRIAL environment variable
    report_root no Project root (default: here::here())
    output_root no Root for redirected output (default: module-local) 
    library(utilrep2)

setup_session(
  platform    = "linux",
  data_path   = "/data/correlates_reporting2/data_clean",
  trial       = "moderna_mock",
  report_root = "/data/correlates_reporting2"
)

    setup_session() prints a status summary and returns the previous values so you can restore the session later:

    old <- setup_session(
  platform  = "win",
  data_path = "/new/data_clean",
  trial     = "janssen_pooled_partA"
)
on.exit(do.call(setup_session, old), add = TRUE)

    Using the mock data from report_root

    The mock datasets live in data_clean/ under report_root and can be used to try the pipeline by pointing data_path there:

    setup_session(
  platform    = "linux",
  data_path   = file.path(report_root, "data_clean"),
  trial       = "moderna_mock",
  report_root = "/path/to/correlates_reporting2"
)

    Available files:

    File Trial label
    moderna_mock_data_processed_with_riskscore.csv moderna_mock
    janssen_pooled_mock_data_processed_with_riskscore.csv janssen_pooled_mock

    Checking the session configuration

    Call session_status() at any time to see what is configured and whether the required settings are in place:

    session_status()
    utilrep2 session configuration
--------------------------------------------------
  ✔  platform       linux
  ✔  data_path      /data/correlates_reporting2/data_clean
  ✔  report_root    /data/correlates_reporting2
  ✔  output_root    (default: inside each module)
  ✔  TRIAL          moderna_mock
--------------------------------------------------

✔ Session is ready.

    If either required setting is missing, the missing items are listed with a warning, and any run_*() call will stop with a clear error:

    ⚠  Required setting(s) missing: platform, data_path
   Call setup_session() to configure.

    Platform settings

    Choosing between Linux and Windows scripts

    Many modules have Windows-compatible rewrites under win/<module>/code/. Tell the package which version to use:

    set_platform("linux")  # use original module code (default)
set_platform("win")    # route all calls through win/<module>/code/
set_platform("auto")   # detect from .Platform$OS.type

get_platform()         # query the effective platform

    Individual run_*() calls can override the package-level setting:

    run_cor_coxph(trial = "moderna_mock", use_win = TRUE)

    Configuring paths

    Data path (required)

    data_path in setup_session() calls set_data_root() internally and sets the CORRELATES_DATA_ROOT environment variable so child Rscript processes pick it up automatically:

    set_data_root("/secure/data/correlates/data_clean")
get_data_root()

    Passing NULL clears the setting (subsequent run_*() calls will then error until a new path is provided):

    set_data_root(NULL)

    Report root (optional)

    By default, module paths are resolved from here::here(). Override when running from a different working directory, a Slurm job, or a network drive:

    set_report_root("/data/projects/correlates_reporting2")
get_report_root()

set_report_root(NULL)   # revert to auto-detection

    Output root (optional)

    By default each module writes results into its own output/ subdirectory. Redirect all output to a separate tree with set_output_root():

    set_output_root("/results/correlates/2026-03-04")

# Inspect the resolved output directory for a specific module
module_output_dir("cor_coxph", "moderna_mock")
# => "/results/correlates/2026-03-04/cor_coxph/output/moderna_mock"

set_output_root(NULL)   # revert to default (output inside each module)

    Next steps

    • See vignette("running-pipelines", package = "utilrep2") to learn how to run full analysis pipelines and build reports.
    • See vignette("os-compatibility", package = "utilrep2") to audit the repository for Windows compatibility issues.
    • Browse the module-* vignettes for step-by-step instructions for each individual analysis module.

    Developed by Y H.
